features.featureTitle: In CharacterSelectScreen.tsx, audit the 'Enter The Realm' button's onClick handler end-to-end. Identify and fix every reason the button produces zero feedback and does not navigate when clicked. Specifically: (1) confirm the button is not conditionally disabled or rendered without an onClick; (2) confirm the handler calls the onSelectCharacter (or equivalent) prop with the selected character's ID; (3) confirm no outer element is swallowing the click event (e.g. a pointer-events-none overlay or a stopPropagation call); (4) confirm the selected character state is non-null at the time the button is clicked so the ID passed is valid; (5) remove any guard or early-return that silently prevents the callback from firing.

In App.tsx, audit the onSelectCharacter callback (or equivalent) that is passed as a prop to CharacterSelectScreen. Verify: (1) the callback is defined as a stable function (useCallback or equivalent) and is not undefined at render time; (2) it correctly writes the selected character's ID into App-level state (e.g. activeCharacterId); (3) after setting the ID it immediately updates the routing state to transition to the character sheet / dungeon select screen; (4) no intermediate loading flag, isLoading guard, or conditional render silently blocks the screen transition after the callback fires; (5) if any such guard exists, ensure it evaluates to false (i.e. not blocking) by the time the callback completes.
